title: 中考六选五 20: The Value of Asking Better Questions
passage:
Many students ask questions when they do not understand something. This is a good habit. 1. ______ A better question can often lead to a better answer.

A weak question is usually too general. For example, “I don’t understand this” does not tell the teacher where the problem is. 2. ______ The teacher may need to guess what the student really means.

A better question is more specific. A student might ask, “Why do we use the past tense here?” or “How do I know which paragraph gives the answer?” 3. ______ Such questions make it easier for teachers to give useful help.

Asking better questions also helps students think more clearly. Before asking, they need to find out what they already know and where they are stuck. 4. ______ This process itself can improve understanding.

Of course, students should not be afraid of imperfect questions. A question does not have to sound clever. 5. ______ The important thing is to move from confusion toward clearer thinking.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: It only needs to be honest and connected to the real problem.
But not all questions are equally helpful.
This forces them to organize their thoughts first.
These questions point to the exact difficulty.
A question like this is hard to answer clearly.
Students should wait until they fully understand before asking questions.
